perturbation.original_text: Carpenter Technology Corporation ("we", "our" or "us") has requested information regarding Talley Industries, Inc. (the "Company", "your" or "you") in connection with our consideration of the possible acquisition of the Company (a "Possible Transaction").
perturbation.changed_text: Carpenter Technology Corporation ("Carpenter", "our" or "us") has requested information regarding Talley Industries, Inc. (the "Organization", "your" or "you") in connection with our consideration of the possible acquisition of the Organization (a "Potential Deal").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Carpenter Technology Corporation' to 'Carpenter', 'Company' to 'Organization', and 'Possible Transaction' to 'Potential Deal' introduces ambiguity. While the intention might be clear from context, the contract now uses different terms for the same entities/events without explicitly defining them as interchangeable. This contradicts the assumption that 'Carpenter' and 'Company' are the official names.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Carpenter Technology Corporation ("Carpenter", "our" or "us") has requested information regarding Talley Industries, Inc. (the "Organization", "your" or "you") in connection with our consideration of the possible acquisition of the Organization (a "Potential Deal"). In consideration of your furnishing us with the Evaluation Materials (as defined below) we agree as follows: Confidentiality of Evaluation Materials We will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Organization or its financial advisor, J.P. Morgan & Co. Incorporated ("JPM"), or the Organization's other representatives furnish to us in connection with a Potential Deal involving the Organization, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by us, or by our representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or our review of, or interest in, the Organization (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials").
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines key terms like 'Company' to 'Organization' and 'Possible Transaction' to 'Potential Deal' while also renaming 'Carpenter Technology Corporation' to 'Carpenter'. The contradicted text then uses these newly defined terms ('Organization', 'Potential Deal', and 'Carpenter') within the same context as the original terms were intended, creating ambiguity and a direct conflict. The contract now uses different terms for the same entities/events without explicitly defining them as interchangeable, leading to confusion about which terms should be enforced and conflicting obligations. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', therefore it's a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential".
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Evaluation Documents" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential".
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Evaluation Materials" to "Evaluation Documents" without amending subsequent references causes inconsistency. The agreement now refers to two potentially distinct sets of documents, creating confusion about which terms and obligations apply to what.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Evaluation Documents" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential". The term "Evaluation Documents" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us or our representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Organization or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Organization or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by us.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Evaluation Materials", while the changed text defines "Evaluation Documents". The explanation states that this change causes inconsistency because subsequent references may use both terms, leading to confusion. The contradicted text refers to "Evaluation Documents" and provides an exclusion list. By changing the initial definition, the document now refers to two potentially distinct sets of documents, creating confusion about which terms and obligations apply to what, hence creating a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: We will not use any to the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: We will not utilize any of the confidential documents for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Potential Deal.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Evaluation Materials' to 'confidential documents' creates ambiguity. While 'Evaluation Materials' are defined, 'confidential documents' is not, potentially narrowing the scope of protection to only those documents explicitly marked as confidential. The original definition for the `Evaluation Materials` refers to regardless of such information is specifically identified as “confidential”.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We will not utilize any of the confidential documents for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Potential Deal. We and our representatives will keep the confidential documents completely secret; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Potential Deal between us and the Organization (it being understood that our representatives shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Organization consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Evaluation Materials' to 'confidential documents' introduces a contradiction because 'confidential documents' is not defined, while 'Evaluation Materials' has a specific definition encompassing information regardless of confidentiality marking. The contradicted text repeats the changed text and further uses the undefined term 'confidential documents'. This undermines the scope of confidentiality intended by the original text, as the protection may now only extend to explicitly marked documents. The original definition included protection irrespective of whether the information was specifically marked as confidential. Therefore, the change narrows the scope and creates a direct conflict regarding what information is protected.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Data Collection, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Organization of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Organization on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Organization in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Evaluation Materials' with 'Data Collection' introduces a new undefined term. This creates uncertainty about what data is subject to the disclosure obligations, potentially leading to disputes over whether certain documents or information fall under this provision. The original definition for the `Evaluation Materials` is not 'Data Collection'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Data Collection, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Organization of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Organization on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Organization in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained or that the Organization waives compliance with the provisions hereof, (i) we or our representatives, as the case may be, may disclose to any tribunal only that portion of the Data Collection which we are advised by counsel is legally required to be disclosed, and shall exercise our best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Data Collection and (ii) we shall not be liable for such disclosure unless disclosure to any such tribunal was caused by or resulted from a previous disclosure by us or our representatives not permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Materials' with 'Data Collection' and renames the 'Company' to 'Organization.' According to the perturbation explanation, 'Data Collection' is a new undefined term, different from the original 'Evaluation Materials.' This undefined term creates uncertainty, potentially leading to disputes. The contradicted text uses this new undefined term 'Data Collection' assuming that the reader knows what it is. Because the original text contains 'Evaluation Materials', and the Changed text replaced this with an undefined term 'Data Collection', this creates a strong in-text contradiction. In addition, since the definition for Evaluation Materials isn't Data Collection, this makes it a strong contradiction because the replaced term isn't a correct synonym, causing further confusion on the meaning of the agreement and the enforceability.
